title: Heaps, Priority Queues, and Scheduling · Invariant Triage
summary: Schedule incidents by urgency, arrival, and changing operational priority. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a stable priority scheduler with heap invariants and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate complete-tree shape to array indices and parent-child heap order.
Trace sift-up, sift-down, push, pop, replace, and heapify costs.
Add stable tie breakers without comparing unrelated payload objects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
